pattern: A shared per-unit weight turns a loading-capacity question into simply dividing total capacity by unit weight, capped at the physical space available.
hints: Every container weighs exactly the same, so the total weight only depends on how many you load, not which cells you choose.
There are two independent limits: the number of physical cells on the deck, and how many unit-weight containers the maximum weight allows.
The answer is the smaller of the deck's cell count (n*n) and the maximum weight divided by the unit weight, rounded down.

edge_inputs.description: max weight smaller than a single container's weight
weight capacity exactly divides evenly with no remainder
huge weight capacity capped by deck cell count
weight cap allows exactly one fewer than the cell count
unit weight equal to max weight allows exactly one container

stress.description: random small ships across the full parameter range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    w = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    maxWeight = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    return [n, w, maxWeight]
